mission.title: Install and Configure NATS
mission.description: NATS is a high-performance messaging system designed for cloud-native applications. It is ideal for microservices architectures, IoT, and edge computing due to its lightweight nature and support for multiple client languages. Key trade-offs include the need for careful configuration to ensure security and performance at scale.

steps.title: Add NATS Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official NATS Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install NATS using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add nats https://nats-io.github.io/k8s/helm/charts

```

> This URL points to the official NATS Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install NATS Server
steps.description:
This command installs the NATS server using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install nats nats/nats --namespace nats --create-namespace --version 2.11.0

```

> Replace '2.11.0' with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create NATS Configuration Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource for NATS configuration, allowing you to specify settings for the NATS server.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a NATS cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify NATS Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the NATS pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> You should see 3 NATS pods in the Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall NATS Server
uninstall.description:
This step removes the NATS server and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all NATS data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall nats --namespace nats

kubectl delete namespace nats

```

> This command will remove the NATS release.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade NATS Server
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your NATS configuration. This step will upgrade the NATS server to a new version.

```bash

kubectl get nats my-nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

helm upgrade nats nats/nats --namespace nats --version 2.11.0

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Backup the current NATS configuration.
> Replace '2.11.0' with the desired version.
> Verify that the NATS pods are running with the new version.
commands: kubectl get nats my-nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: NATS pods are in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the NATS server settings.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the NATS pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n nats' and correct the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: NATS service is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the service is not properly exposed or the network policies are blocking access.

**Fix:**
Ensure the NATS service is of type 'ClusterIP' or 'LoadBalancer' and check network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to NATS from client
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Client libraries may not be compatible with the NATS server version.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the client library version matches the NATS server version.

**Affected versions:** <= 2.10

troubleshooting.title: NATS server is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to insufficient resources allocated to the NATS pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the NATS deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for JetStream, improved performance for large message payloads.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.10, review the JetStream documentation for configuration changes.

resolution.summary: You should see the NATS pods running with the output similar to 'my-nats-0, my-nats-1, my-nats-2' in the 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n nats'.

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
